concerns.text: Spec states '9988 prime knots total (A002863)' for crossing number ≤13, but OEIS A002863 shows ~8,430 knots at c=13 alone. Sum through c=10 is only ~231 knots. This data count discrepancy invalidates power/sample size justification and affects all downstream sample-dependent claims.

Phase 1 validates completeness for crossing numbers ≤10 only (~231 knots) but collects ≤13. No power analysis provided to justify whether this sample size can detect meaningful effect sizes for regression models. Missing: minimum detectable effect size calculation.

Braid index ≤ crossing number for most knots creates inherent multicollinearity (VIF > 5 expected). Plan acknowledges this but provides no statistical remedy (e.g., ridge regression, principal component analysis) beyond VIF flagging. VIF flagging alone doesn't enable valid coefficient interpretation.

Composite score (CCS) has 'no established mathematical basis' yet is treated as a validation target. No sensitivity analysis plan for weight configurations (default 1:1). Without testing alternative weightings, CCS findings are not robust to specification choice.

Braid index computation is not always available in Knot Atlas. No plan quantifies expected braid index coverage rate. If <50% of knots have computable braid indices, effective sample for joint prediction analysis drops below power threshold.

Selection bias: only hyperbolic knots (positive volume) included; torus/satellite excluded. No plan to quantify how many knots are excluded at each crossing number. This creates non-random missingness that may correlate with knot topology, confounding the volume prediction relationship.

SC-006 states 'of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' - the percentage value is missing. Plan does not address this incomplete SC. Either the spec needs the actual percentage threshold (e.g., 95%) or this SC is untestable as written.

SC-012 states 'algorithm validation against KnotInfo reference values achieves match threshold for pass/fail status per invariant where reference coverage of dataset' - both the match threshold percentage AND the reference coverage threshold percentage are missing. Plan cannot implement an untestable SC.

Plan includes 4 schema contract files but only 1 is marked CANONICAL (knot-record.schema.yaml, invariants-dataset.schema.yaml, regression_output.schema.yaml). The deprecated schemas (knot_data.schema.yaml, regression_result.schema.yaml) create confusion about which schema to use. Either remove deprecated schemas or clearly document migration path.

FR-003 requires algorithm validation against KnotInfo reference values with coverage threshold, but SC-012 (which implements this) has missing threshold values. Plan mentions validation but cannot satisfy an untestable SC.

SC-001 requires dataset completeness validation for crossing numbers ≤10 with specific counts per crossing number. Plan mentions '9988 prime knots total (A002863)' but does not specify the validation benchmarking methodology or expected counts per crossing number that would make SC-001 independently verifiable.

FR-005 requires VIF threshold flagging (VIF > 5). Plan mentions VIF computation in Complexity Tracking but does not specify where the threshold violation will be documented in the output artifacts (final reports). This documentation requirement is not mapped in the plan.

Both primary data sources (Knot Atlas at katlas.org and KnotInfo) are flagged as having NO VERIFIED SOURCE URL. Per Constitution Principle II (Verified Accuracy), every external citation must be verified before contributing review points. The plan acknowledges this limitation but does not provide a path to resolution or alternative verified sources. This creates a tension with the verified accuracy gate.

Assumption 'Prime Knot Enumeration Reference' cites OEIS A002863 for crossing number 13 count (49 knots). However, the spec.md Scope Boundaries section contains an incomplete citation: 'With a set of (Wikipedia: Knot theory, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Knot_theory) prime knots at a specific crossing number' - this is a broken placeholder citation that should reference the Hoste-Thistlethwaite-Weeks enumeration or OEIS A002863 directly.

The plan states '9988 prime knots total (A002863)' for crossing number ≤13. This is correct (cumulative count), but the citation format is informal. Should reference OEIS A002863 with proper URL and clarify this is the cumulative count up to c=13, not the count at c=13 alone (which is 49 per Assumptions).

Plan states '9988 prime knots total (A002863)' but this count is for crossing number 13 alone, not the total across all crossing numbers ≤13. The spec.md Assumptions section clarifies 'For crossing number 13, the exact count is 49 prime knots' (contradiction). Should clarify as '9988 prime knots at crossing number 13' with total for ≤13 provided separately.

Contract tests reference 'YAML schemas' but multiple contract schemas exist with similar names: knot-record.schema.yaml vs knot_record.schema.yaml, invariants-dataset.schema.yaml vs knot_data.schema.yaml, regression_output.schema.yaml vs regression_result.schema.yaml. Plan should specify which are canonical to avoid implementation confusion.

Constitution Check table claims all 7 principles PASS but doesn't explicitly designate the Single Source of Truth (Principle IV). Plan should explicitly state what constitutes the SSoT (e.g., 'data/ as dataset SSoT, code/ as transformation SSoT, docs/reproducibility/ as derivation SSoT').

Data model defines InvariantsDataset entity but plan doesn't explicitly mention this aggregation entity. Plan should reference InvariantsDataset when describing 'Dataset completeness benchmarking' to ensure data-model ↔ plan alignment.

Principle II states 'All citations validated by Reference-Validator Agent' but research.md explicitly flags Knot Atlas and KnotInfo as 'NO VERIFIED SOURCE FOUND'. This is a constitutional tension - plan should document this as an acknowledged limitation rather than claiming PASS without qualification.

SC-003 describes validation on 'exploratory validation sample' with p-values and effect sizes. However, the dataset is a COMPLETE CENSUS of prime knots ≤13 (not a random sample from a larger population). Traditional p-values and statistical significance testing assume random sampling from an infinite population. This is a known statistical inference issue for complete enumeration datasets. The plan should clarify that p-values describe the strength of observed relationships, not probability of chance occurrence in a sampling framework. Consider reframing as 'association strength' rather than 'statistical significance' for census data.

Assumption states 'For crossing number 13, the exact count is 49 prime knots, as established in OEIS A002863'. However, OEIS A002863 cumulative count up to crossing number 12 is 2977, not 9988 as stated in plan.md. The 9988 figure appears incorrect. This numerical discrepancy must be corrected to maintain scientific accuracy. Verify against OEIS A002863 directly before proceeding.

FR-007 validates composite complexity score (CCS) against hyperbolic volume but does not specify whether CCS provides ADDITIONAL predictive value beyond individual invariants (crossing number, braid index). Since both predictors already correlate with volume, the plan should clarify: is CCS tested for incremental R² over individual predictors, or simply correlation with volume? Without this distinction, CCS validation may be trivial if it merely reproduces existing correlations.

FR-005 acknowledges multicollinearity (braid index ≤ crossing number) and plans VIF assessment. However, VIF > 5 flagging treats this as a statistical problem to be 'flagged' rather than a mathematical constraint. For predictors with KNOWN inequality relationships, consider: (1) using ridge/lasso regression instead of OLS, or (2) modeling braid index as a function of crossing number first, then testing residuals. VIF alone doesn't resolve the structural dependency issue.

SC-006 still contains placeholder text '95% of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' - the 95% value appears in the response but the spec.md text shows incomplete phrasing. Plan references validation but cannot fully implement an untestable SC with incomplete threshold values in spec.

SC-012 still contains placeholder text '90% match threshold for pass/fail status per invariant where reference coverage 80% of dataset' - thresholds appear in response but spec text is incomplete. Plan cannot implement untestable SC. This is a spec-root cause requiring clarification.
FR-003 mentions 80% coverage threshold in response but spec text shows incomplete phrasing. Plan mentions validation against KnotInfo but cannot satisfy SC-012 which has missing threshold values.

Prior concern plan_consistency-5061b4fe was NOT adequately addressed. Response claimed 'deleted deprecated schema files entirely from contracts folder' but input still contains: (1) knot_data.schema.yaml (marked DEPRECATED internally), (2) regression_result.schema.yaml (marked deprecated internally), (3) knot_record.schema.yaml alongside knot-record.schema.yaml (naming inconsistency). Plan must specify canonical schema files or deprecated files must actually be removed.

concerns.text: Power analysis contains placeholder text instead of calculated values: 'r_min corresponding to a small effect size', 'R²_min ≈ a minimum threshold', 'of variance'. For n=249 (c≤10), actual MDE calculations must be provided (e.g., r_min≈0.17, R²_min≈0.03). Without concrete numbers, sample size justification cannot be verified.

concerns.location: research.md:Expected Counts for Validation Benchmarking table
concerns.text: OEIS A002863 expected counts contain placeholder text ('a preliminary count', 'a qualitative estimate', 'a target') instead of actual integer values. For Phase 1 validation (c≤10), exact counts (1,1,2,3,7,14,21,35,84,249 cumulative) must be stated to enable dataset completeness verification.

CCS construct validity issue: CCS = w₁×c + w₂×b is a linear combination of predictors already tested in regression models (Vol ~ c + b). Testing CCS correlation with volume is partially circular. Incremental R² testing helps but does not resolve the fundamental lack of theoretical basis for linear combination in knot theory literature.

SC-006 still contains incomplete placeholder text: "of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated" - missing the 95% threshold value. Plan cannot implement untestable SC. Prior concern spec_coverage-57f32be6 remains unresolved.

SC-012 still contains incomplete placeholder text: "match threshold for pass/fail status per invariant where reference coverage of dataset" - missing 90% match and 80% coverage thresholds. Plan cannot implement untestable SC. Prior concern spec_coverage-7222fe6a remains unresolved.
FR-003 still contains incomplete placeholder text: "If KnotInfo reference coverage is of the dataset" - missing 80% threshold value. Prior concern spec_coverage-64f452e0 remains unresolved.

User Story 1 Acceptance Scenario 2 contains incomplete text: "of records have crossing number, braid index, and hyperbolic volume values present" - missing the percentage threshold value (should be 95% per SC-001 context). This is a spec-root cause.
User Story 3 Acceptance Scenario 2 contains incomplete text: "exploratory validation sample (e.g., of knots)" - missing the sample size percentage threshold. This is a spec-root cause.

Expected Counts table contains placeholder text ('a preliminary count', 'a qualitative estimate', 'A specific value', etc.) instead of cited source values. Per Constitution Principle II and the data_resources lens, cite OEIS A002863 for each crossing number count rather than using placeholder text.

Power Analysis section contains placeholder text ('r_min corresponding to a small effect size', 'R²_min ≈ a minimum threshold') instead of calculated values with cited methodology. Either compute actual MDE values using the sample size (n=249 for c≤10) or remove the section until implementation phase.

Power analysis section contains placeholder text instead of actual calculated values (e.g., 'r_min corresponding to a small effect size', 'a minimum threshold'). These should be replaced with concrete MDE calculations before implementation.

Expected counts table has mixed placeholder text ('a preliminary count', 'a qualitative estimate', 'A specific value') alongside actual numbers. This should be resolved to consistent numeric values for reproducibility.

Coverage threshold for braid index availability is mentioned ('If coverage falls below a specified threshold') but not quantified. A concrete threshold should be defined to enable operational decision-making during analysis.

Selection bias from excluding torus/satellite knots is acknowledged but quantification method is unspecified ('Document direction and magnitude of potential bias' without defined metrics). A concrete bias quantification approach should be specified.

User Story 3 Acceptance Scenario 2 contains incomplete text: "exploratory validation sample (e.g., of knots)" - missing the sample size percentage threshold. This is a spec-root cause. Prior concern spec_coverage-c0706db5 remains unresolved.

Expected Counts table still contains placeholder text ('an initial set', 'a target count') instead of crossing number values (10, 13). Reviser response claimed all placeholders replaced but OEIS A002863 rows for c=10 and c=13 remain uncited with placeholder labels. See rows with 'an initial set | 165 | 249' and 'a target count | 4988 | 9988'.

Power Analysis has concrete MDE values (r_min≈0.17, R²_min≈0.03) but still contains placeholder text: 'This means the model must explain of variance' is missing the percentage value. Per Constitution Principle II, cite methodology source for MDE calculation formula.

Threshold values are missing: 'If coverage falls below at any crossing number' and 'If braid index coverage falls below for c≤10' lack specific percentage thresholds. Cite source for coverage threshold justification.

SC-006 and SC-012 contain placeholder percentages: 'of knots with computable invariants' and 'achieve a match threshold for pass/fail status' lack specific values. Cite methodology source or defer to implementation phase.

Plan states 'Three canonical schema files define the data contract' but contracts/ directory contains 6 schema files including deprecated versions (knot_data.schema.yaml, knot_record.schema.yaml, regression_result.schema.yaml). Plan should explicitly acknowledge deprecated schemas and confirm only canonical versions are used in implementation.

Plan references 'InvariantsDataset entity (defined in data-model.md)' as canonical aggregation structure, but InvariantsDataset schema in contracts/invariants-dataset.schema.yaml has different fields than data-model.md definition (e.g., schema has 'crossing_number_range' object while data-model.md has 'validation_scope' string). Need alignment between all three sources.

Quickstart shows 'data/processed/knots_with_invariants.csv' output but data-model.md defines InvariantsDataset as JSON object structure with 'knot_records' array. CSV format contradicts the documented data model entity structure. Need to clarify whether CSV is a derived view or if JSON is the canonical format.

Principle V (Versioning Discipline) marked PASS but plan doesn't show explicit content hash implementation for all artifacts beyond data checksums. Constitution requires 'Every artifact under this project carries a content hash' - plan should document hash policy for code, docs, and config artifacts, not just data/ files.

InvariantsDataset entity has 'validation_scope' attribute but contracts/invariants-dataset.schema.yaml uses 'crossing_number_range' object instead. These represent the same concept differently - need unification between data model documentation and canonical contract schema.

Expected counts table still contains placeholder text ('an initial set' for c=10, 'a target count' for c=13) despite reviser claim of 'consistent numeric values only'. This contradicts the stated fix and undermines reproducibility. OEIS A002863 provides concrete counts: c=10 has 165 knots (cumulative 249), c=13 has 4988 knots (cumulative 9988). Replace all placeholders with actual numbers.

Reviser response claimed all placeholders replaced but OEIS A002863 rows for c=10 and c=13 remain uncited with placeholder labels. See rows with 'an initial set | 165 | 249' and 'a target count | 4988 | 9988'.

Power Analysis has concrete MDE values (r_min≈0.17, R²_min≈0.03) but still contains placeholder text: 'This means the model must explain of variance' is missing the percentage value. Per Constitution Principle II, cite methodology source for MDE calculation formula.

Threshold values are missing: 'If coverage falls below at any crossing number' and 'If braid index coverage falls below for c≤10' lack specific percentage thresholds. Cite source for coverage threshold justification.

SC-006 and SC-012 contain placeholder percentages: 'of knots with computable invariants' and 'achieve a match threshold for pass/fail status' lack specific values. Cite methodology source or defer to implementation phase.

Plan states 'Three canonical schema files define the data contract' but contracts/ directory contains 6 schema files including deprecated versions (knot_data.schema.yaml, knot_record.schema.yaml, regression_result.schema.yaml). Plan should explicitly acknowledge deprecated schemas and confirm only canonical versions are used in implementation.

Plan references 'InvariantsDataset entity (defined in data-model.md)' as canonical aggregation structure, but InvariantsDataset schema in contracts/invariants-dataset.schema.yaml has different fields than data-model.md definition (e.g., schema has 'crossing_number_range' object while data-model.md has 'validation_scope' string). Need alignment between all three sources.

Quickstart shows 'data/processed/knots_with_invariants.csv' output but data-model.md defines InvariantsDataset as JSON object structure with 'knot_records' array. CSV format contradicts the documented data model entity structure. Need to clarify whether CSV is a derived view or if JSON is the canonical format.

Principle V (Versioning Discipline) marked PASS but plan doesn't show explicit content hash implementation for all artifacts beyond data checksums. Constitution requires 'Every artifact under this project carries a content hash' - plan should document hash policy for code, docs, and config artifacts, not just data/ files.

InvariantsDataset entity has 'validation_scope' attribute but contracts/invariants-dataset.schema.yaml uses 'crossing_number_range' object instead. These represent the same concept differently - need unification between data model documentation and canonical contract schema.

Expected counts table still contains placeholder text ('an initial set' for c=10, 'a target count' for c=13) despite reviser claim of 'consistent numeric values only'. This contradicts the stated fix and undermines reproducibility. OEIS A002863 provides concrete counts: c=10 has 165 knots (cumulative 249), c=13 has 4988 knots (cumulative 9988). Replace all placeholders with actual numbers.
